# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Define a dictionary for storing the user-defined plugins paths in environment variables 
let configModulePaths = {};  
if (process.env['CONFIG_MODULE_PATHS']) { // check if CONFIG_MODULE_PATHS is defined, it'll be stringified json of objects with property moduleName and path where to load the plugin from on app start up. example: "module1:'/path-to-plugin', 
    configModulePaths = JSON.parse(process.env['CONFIG_MODULE_PATHS']); // parse back into object if it'a defined, this is required because deno deploy runs with environment variables set in the container which are stringified jsons and not javascript objects for us to easily manipulate them
} 
import { serve } from "deno";
function handleRequest(request: Request) { // define what function gets called when a request comes. For this we use Deno's built-in server functionality that takes requests, processes it (handles module loading and dynamic import), then sends back the response 
    let url = new URL(request.url);  
     const pluginName= decodeURIComponent((new URLPattern()).exec(url.pathname)?.[1] || ''); // get query parameter "module" name to load a custom module from plugins directory and find it on request, if not specified default is "" or main  file for the application as per our requirements
     const path= configModulePaths[pluginName ] ?? '';   // accesses environment variable setting in user defined plugin settings where we define modules paths. It'll throw error when module name doesn’t exist like "undefined" if not set otherwise, it defaults to empty string or main file for the application
    try {  const mod = await import(path);      return new Response('Hello Deno Deploy!',{status:200}); } // dynamically load a specific module and send back response which greeting message on successful execution   catch (error) {     console.log("Error encountered", error );      
    throw error;  }}
serve(handleRequest);// serve request to the client when it comes in via "deno deploy" platform for us, otherwse where is my main file which we want user access through API call or UI based configuration etc will get dynamic loaded.   // deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any
